questions.question: What is a benefit of VRRP?

options.A: It provides traffic load balancing to destinations that are more than two hops from the source.
options.B: It provides the default gateway redundancy on a LAN using two or more routers.
options.C: It allows neighbors to share routing table information between each other.
options.D: It prevents loops in a Layer 2 LAN by forwarding all traffic to a root bridge, which then makes the final forwarding decision.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using FHRP (First Hop Redundancy Protocol)?

options.A: balancing traffic across multiple gateways in proportion to their loads
options.B: reduced management overhead on network routers
options.C: reduced ARP traffic on the network

Which action is taken by the router when a packet is sourced from 10.10.10.2 and destined for 10.10.10.16?

options.B: It uses a route that is similar to the destination address
options.C: It floods packets to all learned next hops
options.D: It queues the packets waiting for the route to be learned

questions.question: What is the purpose of using First Hop Redundancy Protocol in a specific subnet?

options.A: forwards multicast hello messages between routers
options.B: sends the default route to the hosts on a network
options.C: filter traffic based on destination IP addressing

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
Router R4 is dynamically learning the path to the server. If R4 is connected to R1 via OSPF Area 20, to R2 via R2 BGP, and to R3 via EIGRP 777, which path is installed in the routing table of R4?

options.A: the path through R2, because the EBGP administrative distance is 20
options.B: the path through R2, because the IBGP administrative distance is 200
options.C: the path through R1, because the OSPF administrative distance is 110
options.D: the path through R3, because the EIGRP administrative distance is lower than OSPF and BGP

What are two benefits of FHRPs? (Choose two)

options.A: They prevent loops in the Layer 2 network.
options.C: They are able to bundle multiple ports to increase bandwidth
options.D: They enable automatic failover of the default gateway.
options.E: They allow multiple devices to serve as a single virtual gateway for clients in the network

questions.question:
Refer to the exhibit.
A network engineer is in the process of establishing IP connectivity between two sites. Routers R1 and R2 are partially configured with IP addressing. Both routers have the ability to access devices on their respective LANs. Which command set configures the IP connectivity between devices located on both LANs in each site?

options.C:
R1
ip route 192.168.1.0 255.255.255.0 GigabitEthernet0/0
R2
ip route 10.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 GigabitEthernet0/0
options.D:
R1
ip route 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.0 GigabitEthernet0/1
R2
ip route 10.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 GigabitEthernet0/1

Which network prefix was learned via EIGRP?

Router R1 resides in OSPF Area 0. After updating the R1 configuration to influence the paths that it will use to direct traffic, an engineer verified that each of the four Gigabit interfaces has the same route to 10.10.0.0/16. Which interface will R1 choose to send traffic to reach the route?
OSPF must be configured between routers R1 and R2. Which OSPF configuration must be applied to router R1 to avoid a DR/BDR election?

options.A:
router ospf 1 
network 192.168.1.1 0.0.0.0 area 0
interface e1/1
ip address 192.160.1.1 255.255.255.252 
ip ospf network broadcast
options.B:
router ospf 1
network 192.168.1.1 0.0.0.0 area 0 
interface e1/1
ip address 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.252 
ip ospf cost 0
options.C:
router ospf 1
network 192.168.1.1 0.0.0.0 area 0 
hello interval 15
interface e1/1
ip address 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.252
options.D:
router ospf 1
network 192.168.1.1 0.0.0.0 area 0 
interface e1/1
ip address 192.168.1.1 255.55.255.252 
ip ospf network point-to-point

Router(config)#interface GigabitEthernet 0/0 
Router(config-if)#ip ospf network point-to-point
